stemHtml: Which of the following gives the correct definition and SI unit for electrical power (P)?

options.a: Electrical power is voltage (V) divided by current (I) (P = V/I). It is measured in watts (W).
options.b: Electrical power is energy multiplied by time (t) (P = Energy x t). It is measured in watts (W).
options.c: Electrical power is energy is divided by time (t) (P = Energy/t). It is measured in watts (W).
options.d: Electrical power is current (I) divided by voltage (V) (P = I/V). It is measured in amperes (A).
